title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/ code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https:// www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

what: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: Tissot Indicatrix characterizes local distortion map projections. package computes plots indicatrixes using PROJ library directly via PROJ R package. Derived (permission) Bill Huber’s GIS StackExchange answer.

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("tissot")

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: tissot() returns tibble distortion properties. second argument projection target; source defaults EPSG:4326: Columns include: scale_h (meridional), scale_k (parallel), scale_a / scale_b (max/min singular values), scale_area, angle_deformation, convergence.
code: library(tissot) tissot(c(147, -42), "+proj=utm +zone=55 +south") #> Tissot indicatrix: 1 point, +proj=utm +zone=55 +south #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>       x     y dx_dlam dy_dlam    dx_dphi dy_dphi #>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1   147   -42 0.74396       0 6.1410e-16 0.99739 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: scale_h <dbl>, scale_k <dbl>, #> #   scale_omega <dbl>, scale_a <dbl>, scale_b <dbl>, #> #   scale_area <dbl>, angle_deformation <dbl>, #> #   convergence <dbl>

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: indicatrix() builds plottable ellipses. dashed circle undistorted reference; filled ellipse shows projection’s distortion. plot chunk utm55 top left indicatrix look like? plot chunk topleft Far UTM zone lot trouble. plot chunk -bad-trouble plot chunk -bad-trouble plot chunk -bad-trouble Map projection arbitrary. plot chunk robinson
code: xy <- expand.grid(x = seq(0, 1e6, length.out = 5), y = seq(4900000, 5700000, length.out = 4)) lonlat <- tissot_unproject(xy, source = "+proj=utm +zone=55 +south") tis <- tissot(lonlat, "+proj=utm +zone=55 +south") plot(indicatrix(tis), scale = 3e4) tissot_map() plot(indicatrix(tis)[1]) ## UTM zone 55 is at 147 longitude (55 * 6 - 183) tis <- tissot(cbind(100, -42), "+proj=utm +zone=55 +south") plot(indicatrix(tis)) ##  In Mercator we have well known problems  tis <- tissot(cbind(147, -42), "+proj=merc") plot(indicatrix(tis)) ## close to the equator Mercator is ok (in exactly the same way that UTM Zone 55 is ok near 147E longitude) tis <- tissot(cbind(147, 0), "+proj=merc") plot(indicatrix(tis)) xy <- expand.grid(seq(-150, 150, by = 30), seq(-60, 60, by = 30)) r <- tissot(xy, "+proj=robin") ii <- indicatrix(r) plot(ii, scale = 6e5, add = FALSE, show.axes  = TRUE, show.circle = TRUE) tissot_map()

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
code: summary(r) #> Tissot indicatrix: 55 points #>   Source CRS: EPSG:4326 #>   Target CRS: +proj=robin #>   Areal scale:  min=0.8154  max=1.2004  mean=1.0095 #>   Angular def:  min=1.9369  max=51.8469  mean=21.3801 deg #>   Scale h:      min=0.8856  max=1.3030  (meridional) #>   Scale k:      min=0.8487  max=1.3555  (parallel)

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: Pass fill.colour ellipses distortion metric: plot chunk coloured plot chunk angle_deformation
code: plot(ii, scale = 6e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map() plot(ii, scale = 6e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "angle_deformation") tissot_map()

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: plot chunk mollweide plot chunk mercator
code: m <- tissot(xy, "+proj=moll") plot(indicatrix(m), scale = 5e5, add = FALSE) tissot_map() merc_xy <- expand.grid(seq(-150, 150, by = 30), seq(-75, 75, by = 15)) me <- tissot(merc_xy, "+proj=merc") plot(indicatrix(me), scale = 5e5, add = FALSE) tissot_map()

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: single indicatrix axes reference circle: plot chunk single2 plot chunk single3
code: ii2 <- indicatrix(c(147, -42), "+proj=lcc +lat_1=-36 +lat_2=-38 +lat_0=-37 +lon_0=145") plot(ii2[[1]], scale = 1e4, add = FALSE, show.axes = TRUE, show.circle = TRUE) ii3 <- indicatrix(c(147, -42), "+proj=lcc +lat_1=-15 +lat_2=5 +lat_0=0 +lon_0=145") plot(ii3[[1]], scale = 1e4, add = FALSE, show.axes = TRUE, show.circle = TRUE)

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: Lambert Conformal Conic plot chunk lcc Universal Transverse Mercator plot chunk utm
code: pxy <- expand.grid(seq(100, 200, by = 25), seq(-75, -45, by = 10)) p <- tissot(pxy, "+proj=lcc +lat_0=-60 +lon_0=147 +lat_1=-70 +lat_2=-55") plot(indicatrix(p), scale = 3e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map() qxy <- expand.grid(seq(100, 200, by = 25), seq(-75, -45, by = 10)) p <- tissot(qxy, "EPSG:32755") plot(indicatrix(p), scale = 3e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map()

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: projection refer regular grid points crs, else get weird situations like , obvious actual pole: plot chunk polar plot chunk laea push centre away pole Azimuthal Equidistant, ’s useful see happens. plot chunk aeqd
code: polar_xy <- expand.grid(seq(-180, 150, by = 30), seq(-80, -50, by = 10)) p <- tissot(polar_xy, "+proj=stere +lat_0=-90 +lon_0=147") plot(indicatrix(p), scale = 2.5e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map() la <- tissot(polar_xy, "+proj=laea +lat_0=-90 +lon_0=147") plot(indicatrix(la), scale = 2.5e5, add = FALSE) tissot_map() lea <- tissot(polar_xy, "+proj=aeqd +lat_0=-20 +lon_0=147") plot(indicatrix(lea), scale = 2.5e5, add = FALSE) tissot_map()

what: Consider generating input in the crs you are assessing
title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: UTM example , using tissot_unproject() usually far better generate grid CRS assessed. grid lon/lat won’t meaningful many projections. plot chunk polar-proj
code: op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) ext <- c(-180, 150, -80, -50) crs <- "+proj=stere +lat_0=-90 +lon_0=147"  projext <- reproj::reproj_extent(ext, crs, source = "EPSG:4326") polar <- expand.grid(seq(projext[1L], projext[2L], by = 30 * 1e5), seq(projext[3L], projext[4L], by = 10 * 1e5)) polar_xy <- tissot_unproject(polar, "EPSG:4326", source = crs) p <- tissot(polar_xy, crs, source = "EPSG:4326") plot(indicatrix(p), scale = 2.5e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map()  ext <- c(-180, 150, -80, -50) crs <- "+proj=laea +lat_0=-90 +lon_0=147" projext <- reproj::reproj_extent(ext, crs, source = "EPSG:4326")  polar <- expand.grid(seq(projext[1L], projext[2L], by = 30 * 1e5), seq(projext[3L], projext[4L], by = 10 * 1e5)) polar_xy <- tissot_unproject(polar, "EPSG:4326", source = crs) p <- tissot(polar_xy, crs, source = "EPSG:4326") plot(indicatrix(p), scale = 2.5e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map() par(op)

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion
text: “Tissot indicatrix” plots ’ll find online just geographic circles drawn map. show happens circle projection, useful — ’s indicatrix. indicatrix Jacobian projection point: gives actual scale factors, angular deformation, areal distortion. package computes . examples: mgimond.

title: tissot - Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion

text: x tissot_tbl, xy-ish input (see tissot()) target target projection CRS (extracted tissot_tbl attributes x one; required otherwise) ... passed tissot() source source CRS (default "EPSG:4326")

title: Build Tissot indicatrix objects — indicatrix
text: indicatrix_list object (list indicatrix objects source target stored attributes)

title: Build Tissot indicatrix objects — indicatrix
text: indicatrix() accepts either: tissot_tbl object (tissot()) — projection extracted attributes, target optional xy-ish input explicit target

title: Build Tissot indicatrix objects — indicatrix
code: ## From a tissot_tbl r <- tissot(cbind(seq(-150, 150, by = 30), 0), "+proj=robin") ii <- indicatrix(r) plot(ii, scale = 5e5)   ## From raw coordinates ii2 <- indicatrix(c(0, 45), "+proj=stere +lat_0=90") plot(ii2)

title: Plot an indicatrix — plot.indicatrix
text: Draws single Tissot indicatrix ellipse current plot. ellipse shows distortion unit circle map projection. Optional overlays include reference unit circle, lambda/phi direction axes.

title: Plot an indicatrix — plot.indicatrix
code: # S3 method for class 'indicatrix' plot(   x,   scale = 1e+05,   n = 72,   col = "#FF990055",   border = "black",   add = TRUE,   show.axes = TRUE,   show.circle = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot an indicatrix — plot.indicatrix
text: x indicatrix object (indicatrix()) scale scaling factor ellipse size projected units n number points ellipse col fill colour ellipse border border colour add logical; add existing plot (default TRUE)? show.axes TRUE, FALSE, named list graphical parameters direction lines. Defaults: list(col.lambda = "red", col.phi = "blue", lwd = 1.5). show.circle TRUE, FALSE, named list graphical parameters reference circle. Defaults: list(col = adjustcolor("white", alpha.f = 1), border = "grey70", lwd = 4.5, lty = 2). ... passed graphics::polygon()

title: Plot an indicatrix — plot.indicatrix
text: input x, invisibly.

title: Plot an indicatrix — plot.indicatrix
text: show.circle show.axes accept TRUE (use defaults), FALSE (hide), named list graphical parameters override defaults. example, show.circle = list(border = "blue", lty = 3).

what: Plot a list of indicatrixes — plot.indicatrix_list
title: Plot a list of indicatrixes — plot.indicatrix_list
text: Draws indicatrixes indicatrix_list, optionally creating new plot adding existing one. Can colour-code fill distortion metric.

title: Plot a list of indicatrixes — plot.indicatrix_list
code: # S3 method for class 'indicatrix_list' plot(   x,   scale = 1e+05,   n = 72,   col = "#FF990055",   border = "black",   add = FALSE,   show.axes = TRUE,   show.circle = TRUE,   fill.by = NULL,   palette = NULL,   ncolors = 64L,   ... )

title: Plot a list of indicatrixes — plot.indicatrix_list
text: x indicatrix_list (indicatrix()) scale scaling factor ellipse size projected units n number points per ellipse col fill colour. single colour, used ellipses. NULL fill.set, colours generated automatically. border border colour add logical; add existing plot? FALSE, creates new plot sized contain ellipses. show.axes TRUE, FALSE, named list graphical parameters. See plot.indicatrix() defaults. show.circle TRUE, FALSE, named list graphical parameters. Default TRUE list method (circle-vs-ellipse comparison makes distortion visible map scale). See plot.indicatrix() defaults. fill.character; name distortion metric colour-code fill. One "scale_area", "angle_deformation", "scale_h", "scale_k", "scale_a", "scale_b". Default NULL (uniform fill). palette colour palette function (default grDevices::hcl.colors()) ncolors number colours palette (default 64) ... passed plot.indicatrix()

title: Plot a list of indicatrixes — plot.indicatrix_list
text: input x, invisibly.

title: Plot a list of indicatrixes — plot.indicatrix_list
code: xy <- expand.grid(seq(-150, 150, by = 30), seq(-60, 60, by = 30)) r <- tissot(xy, "+proj=robin") ii <- indicatrix(r)  ## Uniform fill plot(ii, scale = 6e5, add = FALSE) tissot_map()   ## Colour by areal distortion plot(ii, scale = 6e5, add = FALSE, fill.by = "scale_area") tissot_map()

what: tissot: Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion — tissot-package
title: tissot: Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion — tissot-package
text: Compute visualize 'Tissot Indicatrix' map projections. indicatrix characterizes projection distortion computing scale factors, angular deformation, areal distortion, convergence arbitrary points. Based calculations shared Bill Huber https://gis.stackexchange.com//5075/482. Uses 'PROJ' coordinate transformation distortion factor computation. Developed using method published Snyder, JP (1987) doi:10.3133/pp1395 .

title: tissot: Tissot Indicatrix for Map Projection Distortion — tissot-package
text: Maintainer: Michael Sumner mdsumner@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Michael Sumner mdsumner@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Bill Huber (original algorithm calculations)

title: Compute Tissot indicatrix properties — tissot
text: Compute Tissot indicatrix given longitude/latitude locations map projection. Returns scale factors, angular deformation, convergence, related distortion properties.

title: Compute Tissot indicatrix properties — tissot
code: tissot(   x,   target,   ...,   source = "EPSG:4326",   method = c("proj", "finitediff"),   A = 6378137,   f.inv = 298.257223563,   dx = 1e-04 )

title: Compute Tissot indicatrix properties — tissot
text: x input coordinates — xy-ish object: two-column matrix, data.frame, tibble, list x/y lon/lat components, length-2 numeric vector single point target target projection CRS string (required) ... ignored source source CRS (default "EPSG:4326") method computation method: "proj" (default) uses PROJ::proj_factors(); "finitediff" uses finite-difference Jacobian (Snyder 1987) semi-major axis ellipsoid (default WGS84; method = "finitediff" ) f.inv inverse flattening (default WGS84; method = "finitediff" ) dx finite difference step degrees (default 1e-4; method = "finitediff" )

title: Compute Tissot indicatrix properties — tissot
text: tissot_tbl tibble columns: x (lon), y (lat), dx_dlam, dy_dlam, dx_dphi, dy_dphi, scale_h, scale_k, scale_omega, scale_a, scale_b, scale_area, angle_deformation, convergence. source target CRS strings stored attributes.

title: Compute Tissot indicatrix properties — tissot
text: default (method = "proj") distortion measures computed via PROJ::proj_factors(), calls PROJ C library directly accurate finite differences (particularly tabular piecewise-defined projections).  original finite-difference path based Snyder (1987) — inspired Bill Huber's formulation https://gis.stackexchange.com//5075/482 — preserved method = "finitediff". x assumed contain longitude/latitude values; default source CRS "EPSG:4326". Set source different geographic CRS.

title: Compute Tissot indicatrix properties — tissot
code: tissot(c(0, 45), "+proj=robin") #> Tissot indicatrix: 1 point, +proj=robin #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>       x     y dx_dlam  dy_dlam dx_dphi dy_dphi scale_h scale_k scale_omega #>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1     0    45   0.761 2.78e-12       0   0.933   0.933    1.08        8.15 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: scale_a <dbl>, scale_b <dbl>, scale_area <dbl>, #> #   angle_deformation <dbl>, convergence <dbl> tissot(cbind(seq(-180, 180, by = 30), 0), "+proj=robin") #> Tissot indicatrix: 13 points, +proj=robin #> # A tibble: 13 × 14 #>        x     y dx_dlam dy_dlam dx_dphi dy_dphi scale_h scale_k scale_omega #>    <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> #>  1  -180     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  2  -150     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  3  -120     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  4   -90     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  5   -60     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  6   -30     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  7     0     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  8    30     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #>  9    60     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #> 10    90     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #> 11   120     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #> 12   150     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #> 13   180     0   0.849       0       0   0.961   0.961   0.849        7.10 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: scale_a <dbl>, scale_b <dbl>, scale_area <dbl>, #> #   angle_deformation <dbl>, convergence <dbl>  ## compare methods tissot(c(0, 45), "+proj=robin", method = "proj") #> Tissot indicatrix: 1 point, +proj=robin #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>       x     y dx_dlam  dy_dlam dx_dphi dy_dphi scale_h scale_k scale_omega #>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1     0    45   0.761 2.78e-12       0   0.933   0.933    1.08        8.15 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: scale_a <dbl>, scale_b <dbl>, scale_area <dbl>, #> #   angle_deformation <dbl>, convergence <dbl> tissot(c(0, 45), "+proj=robin", method = "finitediff") #> Tissot indicatrix: 1 point, +proj=robin #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>       x     y dx_dlam dy_dlam dx_dphi dy_dphi scale_h scale_k scale_omega #>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1     0    45    1.07       0       0   0.946   0.946    1.07        7.25 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: scale_a <dbl>, scale_b <dbl>, scale_area <dbl>, #> #   angle_deformation <dbl>, convergence <dbl>

what: Get or set the current plot projection — tissot_get_proj
title: Get or set the current plot projection — tissot_get_proj
text: Deprecated. Prefer passing target explicitly. functions access package-level projection state. Prefer passing target explicitly tissot_map() tissot_abline(), let plot.indicatrix_list() image.tissot_raster() set automatically.

title: Get or set the current plot projection — tissot_get_proj
code: tissot_get_proj()  tissot_set_proj(target)

title: Get or set the current plot projection — tissot_get_proj
text: target projection CRS string

title: Get or set the current plot projection — tissot_get_proj
text: tissot_get_proj() returns current projection string NULL

title: Get or set the current plot projection — tissot_get_proj
code: tissot_set_proj("+proj=robin") tissot_get_proj() #> [1] "+proj=robin" tissot_set_proj(NULL)  # reset

what: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
title: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
text: tissot_map() draws bundled world coastline, projected projection current. projection determined order: explicit target argument projection recorded recent call plot.indicatrix_list(), image.tissot_raster(), tissot_raster()

title: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
code: tissot_map(..., target = NULL, add = TRUE)  tissot_abline(x, y = NULL, ..., source = "EPSG:4326", target = NULL)

title: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
text: ... graphical parameters passed graphics::lines() (adding) graphics::plot() (creating new) target target CRS. NULL, uses last plot projection (plot.indicatrix_list()) draws lon/lat. add logical; add existing plot (default TRUE) create new x longitude values (xy-ish input; see tissot()) y latitude values (ignored x matrix) source source CRS coordinates (default "EPSG:4326")

title: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
text: tissot_map() invisibly returns (projected) world coastline matrix tissot_abline() called side effect

title: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
text: tissot_abline() draws vertical horizontal reference lines given longitude/latitude projected coordinates.

title: Plot world coastline on a projected map — tissot_map
code: r <- tissot(cbind(seq(-150, 150, by = 30), 0), "+proj=robin") ii <- indicatrix(r) plot(ii, scale = 6e5, add = FALSE) tissot_map()

what: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
title: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
text: Create raster-like grid Tissot distortion metrics target projection. grid laid projected coordinates, back-projected longitude/latitude, passed tissot(). Returns list matrices compatible image() easily converted raster objects (e.g. via terra::rast()).

title: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
code: tissot_raster(   target,   extent = NULL,   radius = 2e+07,   nx = 100L,   ny = NULL,   metrics = c("scale_area", "angle_deformation", "scale_h", "scale_k"),   ...,   source = "EPSG:4326" )  # S3 method for class 'tissot_raster' image(x, metric = NULL, col = NULL, asp = 1, main = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'tissot_raster' plot(x, ...)

title: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
text: target target projection CRS string extent numeric length 4: c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) projected coordinates. NULL (default), estimated global lonlat bounding box (clamped radius). radius maximum half-width grid extent projected units (default 2e7). auto-detected extent clamped dimension exceeds 2 * radius. Prevents blow-projections like stereographic gnomonic map whole globe huge coordinates. Ignored extent supplied. nx integer; number grid cells x (default 100) ny integer; number grid cells y. NULL, set maintain approximately square cells. metrics character vector column names tissot() output include layers. Default includes areal scale, angular deformation, meridional/parallel scale factors. ... passed image() source source CRS (default "EPSG:4326") x tissot_raster (tissot_raster()) metric metric plot (default: first available) col colour palette (default: hcl.colors(64, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE)) asp aspect ratio (default 1) main plot title (default: metric name)

title: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
text: tissot_raster list components: x numeric vector x (easting) cell centers y numeric vector y (northing) cell centers z named list ny x nx matrices, one per metric target target CRS string extent c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) used object image() method quick plotting.

title: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
text: extent NULL, function samples dense set boundary coordinates spanning \([-180, 180] \times [-85, 85]\) (longitude/latitude), projects target CRS, uses resulting bounding range (padded 5%) grid extent.

title: Compute distortion surfaces on a regular grid — tissot_raster
code: tr <- tissot_raster("+proj=robin", nx = 60) image(tr)   ## specific metric image(tr, metric = "angle_deformation")   ## with coastline overlay image(tr) tissot_map()   ## polar stereo — use radius to cap extent tp <- tissot_raster("+proj=stere +lat_0=-90", radius = 5e6, nx = 60) image(tp) tissot_map()

what: Unproject coordinates to geographic (lon/lat) CRS — tissot_unproject
title: Unproject coordinates to geographic (lon/lat) CRS — tissot_unproject
text: convenience wrapper around PROJ::proj_trans() converting projected coordinates geographic. Useful generating regular grids projected CRS feed tissot(), requires lon/lat input.

title: Unproject coordinates to geographic (lon/lat) CRS — tissot_unproject
code: tissot_unproject(x, target = "EPSG:4326", ..., source = NULL)

title: Unproject coordinates to geographic (lon/lat) CRS — tissot_unproject
text: x input coordinates — xy-ish object: two-column matrix, data.frame, tibble, list x/y lon/lat components, length-2 numeric vector single point target target CRS string (default "EPSG:4326"). Must geographic. ... ignored source source CRS string (required). Must projected CRS.

title: Unproject coordinates to geographic (lon/lat) CRS — tissot_unproject
text: two-column matrix longitude latitude values.

title: Unproject coordinates to geographic (lon/lat) CRS — tissot_unproject
World coastline — world
text: matrix longitude/latitude coordinates representing simplified world coastline, derived maps package. Continent boundaries separated NA rows. Longitudes constrained abs(lon) <= 180.

title: World coastline — world
code: world

title: World coastline — world
text: two-column numeric matrix (longitude, latitude)

title: tissot 0.3.0
text: tissot() gains method argument (default "proj"). new "proj" method delegates PROJ::proj_factors(), calling PROJ C library directly exact distortion factors rather approximating via finite differences. accurate, especially tabular piecewise projections (e.g. Robinson). original finite-difference computation (Snyder 1987), inspired Bill Huber’s formulation https://gis.stackexchange.com//5075/482, preserved method = "finitediff". PROJ package now sole coordinate transformation dependency; gdalraster removed. tissot_raster() computes distortion surfaces regular projected grid, returning tissot_raster object image() plot() methods. Extent auto-detected global lon/lat bounding box (clamped radius) supplied explicitly. Supports metric [tissot()] output.

title: tissot 0.3.0
text: gdalraster removed Imports. coordinate transformation now goes PROJ::proj_trans() PROJ::proj_crs_text(). CRS validation (srs_is_geographic) handled internal helper using PROJ::proj_crs_text().

title: tissot 0.3.0
text: Fixed default metrics tissot_raster(): names now use underscores ("scale_area", "scale_h", "scale_k") matching actual tissot_tbl column names (previously used dots, causing silently -NA output).

title: tissot 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-12 Major refactor — modernized engine, new API, rich plotting.

title: tissot 0.2.0
text: Projection engine switched reproj transform_xy() gdalraster. projection work now single batched GDAL call. API renamed: proj./proj.→ source/target, following reproj conventions. target second positional argument (required); source defaults "EPSG:4326". tissot() input now “xy-ish” object (matrix, data.frame, list, length-2 vector) via internal as_xy() helper. old lambda/phi positional arguments replaced x. tissot() returns tissot_tbl (subclassed tibble) source target stored attributes. Column names changed : x, y, dx_dlam, dy_dlam, dx_dphi, dy_dphi, scale_h, scale_k, scale_omega, scale_a, scale_b, scale_area, angle_deformation, convergence. indicatrix() returns indicatrix_list (replaces indicatrixes class). Accepts tissot_tbl raw coordinates + explicit target. Removed: indicatrix0(), tissot0(), .prj(), internal numericDeriv path. Jacobian now computed directly via finite differences.

title: tissot 0.2.0
text: plot.indicatrix() plot.indicatrix_list() : Reference unit circle overlay (show.circle) Lambda/phi direction axes (show.axes) show.circle show.axes accept TRUE, FALSE, named list graphical parameters full customization Colour-coded fill via fill.(e.g. "scale_area", "angle_deformation") print() summary() methods tissot_tbl. print(), length(), [ methods indicatrix_list. tissot_map() tissot_abline() accept explicit target argument. as_xy() internal helper coerces diverse coordinate inputs. resolve_gpar() internal helper logical--list graphical parameter pattern.

title: tissot 0.2.0
text: Fully vectorized Jacobian computation — per-point loop. Computation uses 3N batched transform_xy() call. Test suite added (testthat).
